subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Vocabulary Clue Lab and use sentence clues, word matches, and category shelves to solve word mysteries.
lesson.objective: I can use context, synonyms, antonyms, multiple meanings, and categories to understand words.
lesson.concept_explanation: Nearby words can explain an unfamiliar word by giving a definition, synonym, example, contrast, or action. Readers also use sentence context to choose among a word's meanings and group related words.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may choose a known meaning without checking context or may confuse synonyms with antonyms.
lesson.real_world_connection: Vocabulary clues help readers understand stories, informational texts, signs, and conversations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the context, compare word meanings, choose the meaning that fits, and sort words by shared attributes.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the whole passage. Find words or actions that tell more about the target word. Choose a meaning, check it in the passage, and explain which clue supports it.

worked_examples.text: Watch: In The kitten was tiny, so it fit in my hand, tiny means very small.
worked_examples.sentence: The kitten was tiny, so it fit in my hand.

worked_examples.text: Demo: quick and fast are synonyms because they mean nearly the same thing.
Demo: apple, banana, and peach belong in the fruit category.

hint_ladder: Read the whole sentence before choosing a word meaning.
Decide whether the words mean the same, opposite, or belong in a group.
For multiple-meaning words, choose the meaning that fits the sentence.

guided_practice.prompt: Read the passage. Which word means nearly the same as rapid?
hints: Read the whole passage and notice the words near the target.
Look for a definition, example, synonym, opposite, or action that gives a clue.
Try each answer in the passage and choose the one that makes the best sense.
guided_practice.explanation: The word dash shows that Nora moved quickly, so quick is a synonym for rapid.

question_audio.text: Read the passage. Which word means nearly the same as rapid?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.passage: Nora made a rapid dash to catch the rolling ball.
guided_practice.sentence: Nora made a rapid dash to catch the rolling ball.

checkpoint.prompt: Read the passage. What does sturdy mean in this passage?
hints: Read the whole passage and notice the words near the target.
Look for a definition, example, synonym, opposite, or action that gives a clue.

checkpoint.prompt: Read the passage. What does supplies mean in this passage?

checkpoint.explanation: The cricket landed beside the pond, so here cricket names a small jumping insect.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — a small jumping insect fits the passage.

checkpoint.sentence: Dad will bolt the garden gate so the goats cannot push it open.

checkpoint.passage: Dad will bolt the garden gate so the goats cannot push it open.

checkpoint.prompt: Read the passage. What does play mean in this passage?
hints: Read the whole passage and notice the words near the target.
Look for a definition, example, synonym, opposite, or action that gives a clue.
Try each answer in the passage and choose the one that makes the best sense.
checkpoint.explanation: The class watched a story about a dragon, so play means a story acted onstage.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — a story acted onstage fits the passage.
choices: to have fun with a toy
checkpoint.sentence: Our class watched a play about a dragon who learns to share.

question_audio.text: Read the passage. What does play mean in this passage?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.prompt: Read the passage. What does watch mean in this passage?
hints: Read the whole passage and notice the words near the target.
Look for a definition, example, synonym, opposite, or action that gives a clue.
Try each answer in the passage and choose the one that makes the best sense.
checkpoint.explanation: The speaker asks someone to keep an eye on the soup, not read a clock.
checkpoint.feedback: Yes — to keep an eye on fits the passage.
choices: a clock worn on the wrist
checkpoint.sentence: Please watch the soup while I answer the door.

question_audio.text: Read the passage. What does watch mean in this passage?
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.passage: Please watch the soup while I answer the door.

misconception_bank.context_clue_ignored: Learner chooses a meaning without using the sentence clues.
misconception_bank.synonym_antonym_confusion: Learner mixes up same-meaning and opposite-meaning relationships.
misconception_bank.multiple_meaning_confusion: Learner uses a familiar meaning that does not fit the sentence.
misconception_bank.category_attribute_error: Learner sorts by a surface feature instead of the shared category or attribute.

game_theme.mission_intro: Use sentence clues and word relationships to unlock the Vocabulary Clue Lab.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Synonyms and Antonyms
level_banks.focus: use synonyms and antonyms to explain word relationships

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting an English mission about Vocabulary and Context Clues. Look at the story and get ready to read carefully. Think about the sounds, words, sentences, or details that matter. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Vocabulary and Context Clues. Look for the reading or writing pattern on the page. Listen for word parts, important details, or sentence choices that help you decide. Remember to reread before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Vocabulary and Context Clues. Follow the reading or writing step shown on the page. Look back at the words and say the clue that helped. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Vocabulary and Context Clues with support. Read the word, sentence, or passage first. Then use the model to explain your thinking. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Vocabulary and Context Clues. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Vocabulary and Context Clues. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Vocabulary and Context Clues. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your English mission work on Vocabulary and Context Clues. Think about the reading or writing strategy you used today. Keep listening, rereading, and checking your answer. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Vocabulary and Context Clues. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to choose what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
